missions.title: Mastery Module 1: Mendelian inheritance/pedigree and Related Concepts
missions.description: Comprehensive analysis covering Mendelian inheritance/pedigree, deviations from Mendelism (incomplete dominance codominance), multiple alleles and polygenic inheritance and advanced applications.

microtargets.title: Mendelian inheritance/pedigree
ncertAnchors: NCERT Biology Class 12, Chapter: Genetics and Evolution, Section detailing Mendelian inheritance/pedigree
mustKnowFacts: A thorough understanding of Mendelian inheritance/pedigree is frequently appears in NEET examinations.
Applying the core principles of Mendelian inheritance/pedigree correctly is essential for solving integrated numerical and conceptual problems in Genetics and Evolution.

diagrams.name: Visual representation of Mendelian inheritance/pedigree
diagrams.provesOrShows: Morphological or functional aspects of Mendelian inheritance/pedigree

commonTraps: Misinterpreting the advanced application scenarios of Mendelian inheritance/pedigree during high-pressure exam scenarios.
Failing to recall the exact conditions or sign conventions associated with Mendelian inheritance/pedigree.
masteryCriteria: Can accurately solve numericals or identify biological structures related specifically to Mendelian inheritance/pedigree.
Successfully avoids common pitfalls when Mendelian inheritance/pedigree is integrated with other concepts in Genetics and Evolution.
pyqPatterns: NEET PyQs emphasize the application of Mendelian inheritance/pedigree in tricky assertion-reason or statement-based questions.

activeRecallQuestions.question: Explain an application where using the concept of Mendelian inheritance/pedigree can lead to a common student error.
